title: megalastrum subincisum
intro: Megalastrum subincisum is a species of plant in the family Ophioglossaceae, commonly known as the adderstongue fern, due to its distinctive tongue-shaped leaves. The plant is traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. It is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating respiratory issues. Megalastrum subincisum can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions, allowing for its medicinal properties to be utilized in various forms of traditional medicine.
